perturbation.original_text: Reseller shall have fifteen (15) calendar days after the later of (i) the delivery of any of the Products by Diversinet; or (ii) any applicable evaluation period; to inspect the Products and ensure their substantial compliance with the corresponding documentation. Unless Reseller notifies Diversinet within such fifteen (15) day period that any of the Products fail to substantially comply with the relevant documentation, such Products shall be deemed accepted.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller shall have fifteen (15) calendar days after the later of (i) the delivery of any of the Products by Diversinet; or (ii) any applicable evaluation period; to inspect the Products and ensure their substantial compliance with the corresponding documentation. However, for Products delivered in Q4 of any year, Reseller shall only have five (5) calendar days to inspect the Products. Unless Reseller notifies Diversinet within such period that any of the Products fail to substantially comply with the relevant documentation, such Products shall be deemed accepted.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the inspection period for products. The initial text allows 15 days for inspection, but the added sentence reduces it to 5 days for Q4 deliveries. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes because the contract now has conflicting inspection timelines. This contradicts the general acceptance terms outlined earlier in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless otherwise agreed to by the Parties, delivery of the Products shall take place via electronic transmission or by courier, and will take place within two (2) Business Days of a written request or purchase order for software and within five (5) Business Days for a standard appliance. Custom built appliances or software are subject to evaluation and delivery date will be communicated after assessment. "Business Day" shall mean 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. (Eastern Standard Time), Monday through Friday, excluding all statutory holidays observed in the Province of Ontario in Canada. Delivery will be F.O.B. Diversinet's facility. Reseller shall have fifteen (15) calendar days after the later of (i) the delivery of any of the Products by Diversinet; or (ii) any applicable evaluation period; to inspect the Products and ensure their substantial compliance with the corresponding documentation. However, for Products delivered in Q4 of any year, Reseller shall only have five (5) calendar days to inspect the Products. Unless Reseller notifies Diversinet within such period that any of the Products fail to substantially comply with the relevant documentation, such Products shall be deemed accepted. In the event Reseller rejects any of the Products, Reseller shall give Diversinet written notice of rejection, specifying in reasonable detail the basis for such rejection. Diversinet will use commercially reasonable efforts to correct any nonconformance and re-deliver such Products to Reseller.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a specific, conflicting deadline for product inspection in Q4, reducing the inspection period from 15 days to 5 days. This directly contradicts the earlier, more general statement allowing for a 15-day inspection period in the contradicted text. This inconsistency creates a strong contradiction and ambiguity, making it unclear which deadline applies to Q4 deliveries, which can affect enforcement and give rise to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Costs associated with the escrow agreement shall be borne by Reseller.
perturbation.changed_text: Costs associated with the escrow agreement shall be borne by Reseller. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Diversinet shall be responsible for all legal fees associated with establishing and maintaining the escrow account.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding who pays for the escrow agreement. The original statement makes the Reseller responsible for all costs. The added sentence then states that Diversinet will be responsible for legal fees. This creates an in-text contradiction and confusion as to which party will bear which expense regarding the escrow agreement, making the agreement overall contradict itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to execute an escrow agreement, within 90 days of the Effective Date, with a nationally recognized escrow agent with respect to the source code for the Products and name Reseller as a beneficiary. Diversinet will then immediately send a copy of the escrow agreement to Reseller for its counter-signature. Diversinet shall, at least annually, deposit the source code in the escrow account. Costs associated with the escrow agreement shall be borne by Reseller. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Diversinet shall be responsible for all legal fees associated with establishing and maintaining the escrow account. Reseller will be provided with a copy of any deposit correspondence and escrow agent's acceptance thereof. The escrow agreement shall provide, among other terms, that the source code shall be released to Reseller if any of the following events (collectively the "Release Conditions") occurs: (i) Diversinet ceases to support services at levels as stated in Schedule 5 under this Agreement that is not remedied within sixty (60) days after receipt of written notice of such failure; (ii) Diversinet makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or becomes subject to direct control of a trustee, receiver or similar authority, or Diversinet becomes subject to any bankruptcy or insolvency proceeding under federal or state statutes; or (iii) Diversinet suspends or ceases to carry on its business and a receiver, trustee or assignee does not carry on the business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that Diversinet will be responsible for legal fees related to the escrow account, while the original text, which is present in the contradicted text, clearly states that "Costs associated with the escrow agreement shall be borne by Reseller." This creates an in-text contradiction regarding which party is responsible for which costs associated with the escrow agreement, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
